decision: Confirm by 9 May whether to proceed straight to ET1 (deadline 3 July 2026) or open settlement talks first; on either route, draft documents ready by 24 June.
rendered:
Decision: 9 May client call to confirm route. ET1 deadline 3 July 2026 (extended under s.207B ERA 1996). Drafts ready by 24 June whichever way.

What to read
- BHS v Burchell [1980] ICR 303 — three-stage test (genuine belief, reasonable grounds, reasonable investigation)
- Iceland Frozen Foods v Jones [1983] ICR 17 — range of reasonable responses on sanction
- Sainsbury's Supermarkets v Hitt [2003] IRLR 23 — investigation reasonableness; confirm still governs
- Polkey v AE Dayton Services [1987] ICR 142 — procedural unfairness and compensation reduction (Acme will argue this)
- ACAS Code of Practice on Disciplinary and Grievance Procedures — paragraphs on investigator/decision-maker separation and appeal independence
- s.98(2)(b) and s.98(4) ERA 1996; s.207A TULRCA 1992 (uplift up to 25%); s.207B ERA 1996 (stop the clock)

What to draft
- ET1 grounds of claim — focus on procedural breaches under ACAS Code (same manager investigated and chaired hearing; evidence not disclosed in advance; appeal heard by dismissing officer's line manager). Ready in draft 24 June.
- Schedule of loss: basic award £2,100; compensatory ~£10,500 (past £4,100 + future £6,400 capped); ACAS uplift up to 25% on compensatory.
- Without-prejudice letter to Hodgson & Pike: opening at £15,750, willing to settle £8,000–£12,000 plus an agreed reference.
- Witness statement template for client — focus on the BlueLight dinner narrative and Tom Reeves's practice of waiving handbook §4.7.
- Subject Access Request to Acme: investigation file, disciplinary hearing notes, appeal notes, training records on §4.7.

What to chase
- Tom Reeves: trace via LinkedIn / last known address. Witness statement on the pre-approval waiver practice is load-bearing.
- Email chain evidencing the BlueLight dinner being agreed as client entertainment.
- BlueLight contract dated 18 February 2026 — copy already in client's handover note; preserve original.
- Staff handbook version in force on 4 February 2026 (not the current edition).
- Acme's prior handling of similar expense incidents — comparators help on reasonable responses.
- Confirm client's gross weekly pay for capped basic award.

claims.claim: Client decision needed by 9 May; ET1 deadline 3 July 2026 under s.207B extension
Three procedural breaches to plead: investigator/chair overlap, late evidence disclosure, appeal heard by dismissing officer's line manager
Schedule of loss: basic award £2,100, compensatory ~£10,500, up to 25% uplift
Settlement range £8,000–£12,000 plus reference is realistic
Tom Reeves's evidence on waiver-in-practice is critical and he has left the business
BlueLight contract signed 18 February 2026 supporting client entertainment narrative
Burchell, Iceland Frozen Foods, Sainsbury's Supermarkets v Hitt, Polkey are the governing authorities — applied here as the standard reading list for an unfair conduct dismissal
